assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 task files contain the required sections. Task 1: Repository (trustify-backend), Target Branch (main), Description (Add AdvisorySeveritySummary...), Files to Create + Files to Modify, Implementation Notes (7 bullet points referencing specific files), Acceptance Criteria (6 items), Test Requirements (2 items). Task 2: all sections present including Files to Create and Files to Modify. Task 3: all sections present including Files to Modify. Task 4: all sections present including Files to Create and Files to Modify.

assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 10 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: 4 task files found: task-1-advisory-severity-summary-model-and-service.md, task-2-advisory-summary-endpoint.md, task-3-cache-invalidation.md, task-4-integration-tests.md. 4 is within [3, 10].
All 4 tasks have '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' as their first section. Task 1 line 1-2, Task 2 line 1-2, Task 3 line 1-2, Task 4 line 1-2.

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1: 'Dependencies: None'. Task 2: 'Depends on: Task 1'. Task 3: 'Depends on: Task 2'. Task 4: 'Depends on: Task 2' and 'Depends on: Task 3'. Dependency graph: 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4, with 2 -> 4. This is a valid DAG with no cycles.

assertion_results.text: Every task's Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths plausible within the repo-backend.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: All modified files exist in the repo-backend.md manifest: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs, modules/fundamental/Cargo.toml, modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs, modules/ingestor/Cargo.toml, tests/Cargo.toml. All created files are in existing parent directories from the manifest: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs (model/ dir exists), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (endpoints/ dir exists), tests/api/advisory_summary.rs (tests/api/ dir exists).

assertion_results.text: Impact map lists concrete, specific changes (not vague or abstract descriptions)
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lists 5 specific changes: 'Add AdvisorySeveritySummary response model struct to the SBOM model layer', 'Add service method to SbomService that queries the sbom_advisory join table and aggregates advisory severity counts by SBOM ID, deduplicating by advisory ID', 'Add GET /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary endpoint with 5-minute tower-http cache and optional ?threshold query parameter', 'Add cache invalidation hook in the advisory ingestion pipeline to invalidate cached summaries when new advisories are linked to an SBOM', 'Add integration tests covering: successful aggregation, 404 for missing SBOM, threshold filtering, cache behavior, and deduplication'. All are concrete with specific technical details.

assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks contain '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' with exactly one repository name. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: 4 digest files found, one per task. digest-task-1.md: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:ca2f613f27e62475115241615ecb07e52a4e119749023bd4d6fd44e1d43ce1e1' (64 hex chars). digest-task-2.md: 'sha256-md:7b93eed8228d534ccc984b9bd885711916c4c2e65d2242d398200c44cf1a437b' (64 hex chars). digest-task-3.md: 'sha256-md:d94a40a517cb0f06ceb2d62c365f04591123c6e73b4f7f218fa0df36c98f3610' (64 hex chars). digest-task-4.md: 'sha256-md:0fd922df159d8b310895d34ad53f8be13a7abf9003cc0887da7857dc5807dbb3' (64 hex chars). All use the correct marker format with sha256-md: prefix and 64 lowercase hex characters.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: None of the 4 task Implementation Notes contain any convention applicability rationales in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'). The repo-backend.md directory tree includes a CONVENTIONS.md file, and the convention-applicability-rules.md requires that applicable conventions include the rationale 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>.' No task contains 'Per CONVENTIONS.md' references or the prescribed 'Applies:' rationale format. Tasks reference docs/constraints.md sections but not CONVENTIONS.md sections with proper applicability validation.
